commit.message:
Fix return-immediately test: accept any state, only validate timing

Per the A2A spec, returnImmediately means the server should respond
quickly — it has no opinion on what task state is returned. The test
now passes if the response arrives within 3 seconds, regardless of
whether the state is Submitted, Working, or Completed.

Updated across all 7 client test suites: .NET, Go, Python, Java,
JavaScript, Rust, and Swift.

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

All volumes are simulated — no platform-specific APIs — so the example
compiles and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Fix perf regression: limit debug info to relevant subprograms (#8174) (#8177)

PR #7799 added debug info for global variables across all inlined
subprograms, creating O(subprograms x globals) debug instructions. This
caused 2-3x slower compilation for shaders with many globals and
deeply-inlined functions.

Fix: Instead of collecting ALL subprograms for a function (walking every
instruction), only collect subprograms from the alloca's actual users --
the instructions that reference the specific global variable. This
preserves the debug info feature (globals visible in inlined scopes that
use them) while eliminating work for unrelated scopes.

The 'Lower static global into Alloca' pass is 3.8x faster on a stress
test (41ms -> 11ms), and overall debug compilation is ~10% faster.

Fixes #8174

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

EVDI multi-device streaming + dynamic resolution + UI toolbar

## EVDI multi-device allocation
- Replaced broken device discovery with lock-file + sysfs validation
- find_evdi_device() now skips cards already connected by Xwayland/compositor
- Scans /sys/class/drm/cardN-*/status to detect in-use DRM connectors
- Lock acquisition is mandatory — no more fallback to unlocked card0
- Each streamer claims its own EVDI card (card3, card4, etc.)

## Dynamic EDID generation
- Replaced hardcoded 1920x1080 EDID with generate_edid(w, h, fps)
- Virtual monitors now advertise the actual requested resolution
- 2K/QHD devices connect at native resolution instead of being capped at FHD

## Desktop UI cleanup
- Replaced messy inline-styled autoconnect area with proper toolbar section
- Autoconnect all button gets green primary accent, EVDI setup is secondary
- Toolbar hints (disabled reason, Wayland notice) render below buttons cleanly
- Added CSS: toolbar, toolbar-row, toolbar-btn, setup-list, code-block
- EVDI wizard modal now has proper ordered-list styling

## Multi-output layout fix
- auto_configure_evdi_outputs() now processes ALL virtual outputs
- Previously only unmirror/extended the newest connector, leaving others stuck

## Scripts / docs
- Updated initial_device_count=1 -> 4 across all scripts and docs
- New scripts/evdi-wizard.sh interactive setup helper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: structured logging overhaul with log/slog (#42)

Replace all log.Printf/fmt.Printf diagnostic logging with Go 1.21+
log/slog for structured, leveled, filterable logging.

New CLI flags:
- --log-level <debug|info|warn|error> (default: warn)
- --log-file <path> — redirect logs to file, keep stderr clean
- --debug — deprecated alias for --log-level debug (backward compat)

Changes:
- Add internal/logging package with slog setup, EvalLogger helper,
  WithPhase/WithTurn child logger constructors
- Wire --log-level and --log-file as persistent flags on root command
- Replace every log.Printf in eval/engine.go, eval/copilot.go,
  eval/proctracker.go, and eval/workspace.go with structured slog calls
- Every eval session log carries prompt, config, phase, worker fields
- Debug-guarded log calls replaced with slog.Debug (no-op at higher levels)
- Add slog.Warn/Info at key points in main.go (SDK fallback, reviewer
  setup, trend analysis warnings)
- Add logging_test.go with level resolution and setup tests

All existing tests pass. No user-facing output (progress bars, results
on stdout) is changed — only diagnostic logging is affected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: run Playwright E2E tests in browser-app CI job (#97) (#113)

Add steps to the browser-app CI job to install Chromium via Playwright
and execute 'npm run test:e2e' so browser regressions are caught on
every PR.

The webServer in playwright.config.ts starts 'cargo run -- app .' using
the debug build already compiled in the same job, so no extra build step
is needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add jump-to-item button in validation issue detail in syu app (#79) (#112)

When a validation issue's subject is a known spec item ID, show a
'→ View <ID>' button in the IssueDetail panel that calls jumpToItem()
to navigate directly to that spec item.

This creates a tight feedback loop: see a validation problem → click →
inspect the problematic spec item → fix it.
